cases.title: Large infrarenal AAA with suitable proximal neck

patient.presentation: Incidental 6.1 cm infrarenal AAA found during workup for abdominal discomfort.

learningObjectives: Identify key CTA measurements required before EVAR.
Recognize an adequate infrarenal neck for standard EVAR.
Choose appropriate first-line management for an asymptomatic large AAA.
Understand why iliac access matters before endograft delivery.

volume.description: Synthetic NRRD CTA volume loaded by the Rust/Tauri backend for the axial viewer spike.

questions.prompt: What is the most appropriate next management step for this stable patient with a 6.1 cm infrarenal AAA?

choices.label: Discharge with repeat ultrasound in 3 years
Elective aneurysm repair planning after anatomic assessment

hints: A 6.1 cm AAA in a fit patient usually needs repair planning.
questions.explanation: A 6.1 cm infrarenal AAA generally meets criteria for elective repair planning if the patient is an acceptable operative candidate. CTA anatomy determines EVAR suitability.

questions.prompt: Which measurements are important before planning standard EVAR? Select all that apply.
hints: The neck and access vessels are critical.
questions.explanation: EVAR planning requires proximal seal measurements and access assessment, including neck diameter, neck length, angulation, iliac diameters, calcification, and tortuosity.

questions.prompt: A short, severely angulated, thrombus-lined proximal neck may increase the risk of type Ia endoleak after EVAR.
questions.explanation: Poor neck morphology can compromise proximal seal and fixation, increasing the risk of type Ia endoleak or migration.

questions.prompt: For this sample case, enter the approximate maximal aneurysm diameter in cm.
hints: The value is in the clinical stem.
questions.explanation: The case stem describes a 6.1 cm aneurysm. In real CTA workflow, maximal diameter should be measured perpendicular to the vessel centerline when possible.

questions.prompt: Name one major complication that CTA surveillance after EVAR is looking for.
questions.explanation: Surveillance looks for complications such as endoleak, graft migration, limb occlusion, kinking, infection, and aneurysm sac enlargement.

questions.prompt: Using the viewer, measure the maximal transverse diameter of the aortic aneurysm on the axial plane.
hints: Switch to the Axial plane and select the Distance tool.
Place the two endpoints at the outer walls of the aortic lumen at its widest point.
questions.explanation: The maximal transverse diameter on axial CTA is a key EVAR planning measurement. The synthetic NRRD volume in this case represents an approximately 61 mm AAA. Measurements within ±8 mm are accepted to account for section choice and caliper placement variation.
